body:
# crystal-imgui

#### [Crystal][] bindings to [Dear ImGui][imgui], through [cimgui][]

Introduction
------------

This library lets you use [Dear ImGui][imgui] with the [Crystal][] programming language.

[Dear ImGui][imgui] is an immediate-mode library for programming and rendering graphical user interfaces. But note that just by itself it won't directly let you do those things, it needs to be wired up to code that actually handles rendering and input. You can write these yourself or use a library ("backend"):

* [crystal-imgui-sfml][] - backend using [SFML][] (assumed through [CrSFML][]).  
  Please refer to its instructions primarily, as it takes over large parts of the installation.

Documentation
-------------

**[API documentation](https://oprypin.github.io/crystal-imgui/)** is currently missing any actual descriptions, but it cross-links to the well-commented [imgui.h](https://github.com/ocornut/imgui/blob/master/imgui.h) header file.

For guidance specific to Crystal and *crystal-imgui*, see [Usage](#usage).

**Examples** of most possible usages of the library are covered in the extensive [src/demo.cr](src/demo.cr) (entry point [examples/demo_sfml.cr](examples/demo_sfml.cr); note that it requires [crystal-imgui-sfml][]).

There's also [a small self-contained example](https://github.com/oprypin/crystal-imgui-sfml/blob/master/example.cr) from [crystal-imgui-sfml][].

Installation
------------

Following the installation process of [crystal-imgui-sfml][] is recommended instead (it even provides *libcimgui.so* automatically), but continue here if you want an alternate route.

Repeat: **do not** follow these instructions if you're using [crystal-imgui-sfml][] (which in the current state of things you probably are).

*crystal-imgui* actually binds to the [cimgui][] library. The supported version is bundled as a submodule (for other versions you can try your luck by [re-generating sources](#contributing)). This repository needs to have been cloned with submodules:

```bash
git clone --recursive https://github.com/oprypin/crystal-imgui
```

Then you can build [cimgui][]:

```bash
cd cimgui
cmake -DCMAKE_CXX_FLAGS='-DIMGUI_USE_WCHAR32' .
cmake --build .
ln -s cimgui.so libcimgui.so  # or .dylib on macOS
```

Yes, the flag is required (just makes the library play much nicely with Unicode *and* Crystal), and the symlink is also required, because the library ends up as _cimgui.so_ but somehow is referred by both that name and _libcimgui.so_.

### Building a project

Try the example from inside the folder of *crystal-imgui*:

```bash
crystal run examples/test.cr
```

Prior to that you'll need to add *cimgui* to the library search path:

```bash
export LD_LIBRARY_PATH="$(pwd)/cimgui"
export LIBRARY_PATH="$(pwd)/cimgui"
```

For your own project, *crystal-imgui* will be in a subdirectory, so adjust this accordingly.

Usage
-----

Using *crystal-imgui* is agnostic of the [backend](#introduction) for the most part, you invoke those just at the beginning of a frame, to process inputs, and at the end, to do the actual rendering (generally [imgui][] just builds up the things to be drawn until the end).

The API surface follows [Dear ImGui][imgui] itself very closely. Main differences are:

* Functions are lowercased: `ImGui::InputFloat3()` becomes `ImGui.input_float3`.
* Types are all in the module as well: `ImGuiIO` becomes `ImGui::ImGuiIO`.
    * *But* you're welcome to "unpack" those as an opt-in, by running `include ImGui::TopLevel`.
* Enums are namespaced.
* **Important**: To faithfully preserve the APIs of ImGui where its functions accept pointers that are used both for input and output (and for lack of an obvious alternative), *crystal-imgui* also requires pointers to be passed to it in the same way.
    * **However**, since Crystal requires very prohibitive circumstances to be able to take an actual pointer, *crystal-imgui* actually wraps these functions into macros, for which `pointerof(foo.bar)` is just a fake syntax that will be transformed into a call to `foo.bar` for reading and a call to `foo.bar=(value)` for writing. As another example, `pointerof(arr[0])` also works - rewritten as `arr[0] = value`.
    * If the input kind expects N values, then a `Slice` of N values should be passed directly instead.
    * For editable strings, one should pass an `ImGui::TextBuffer` instead.
* String buffers (particularly begin+end pairs) are abstracted as `Slice` or `ImGui::TextBuffer`.
    * For the latter, *crystal-imgui* even takes the liberty to implement the buffer resize callback for unbounded text inputs.
* `ImVector` is discouraged. Usually you can just work with `Array` or `Slice`.
* `ImColor` is even more discouraged, in favor of `ImVec4`. See some color helper functions in _src/imgui.cr_.

Contributing
------------

Note that most source files of *crystal-imgui* are auto-generated from outputs of [cimgui][].

If you're editing files, make sure they wouldn't be overwritten by `generate.cr` - in that case that is the file that you should be editing instead. In general, to generate the source code, run this:

```bash
crystal generate.cr  # assumes cimgui/ is populated, writes to src/
crystal tool format src
```

Credits
-------

*crystal-imgui* was made by [Oleh Prypin][oprypin]. It uses and is based on [Dear ImGui][imgui] and [cimgui][].

*crystal-imgui* is [licensed](LICENSE.md) under the terms and conditions of the *MIT* license.


[imgui]: https://github.com/ocornut/imgui
[cimgui]: https://github.com/cimgui/cimgui
[sfml]: https://www.sfml-dev.org/ "Simple and Fast Multimedia Library"
[crystal-imgui-sfml]: https://github.com/oprypin/crystal-imgui-sfml
[crsfml]: https://github.com/oprypin/crsfml

[crystal]: https://crystal-lang.org/

[oprypin]: https://github.com/oprypin

class_methods.name: accept_drag_drop_payload
class_methods.doc:
accept contents of a given type. If ImGuiDragDropFlags_AcceptBeforeDelivery is set you can peek into the payload before the mouse button is released.

[ImGui::AcceptDragDropPayload()](https://github.com/ocornut/imgui/blob/v1.81/imgui.h#L758)
class_methods.args_string: (type : String, flags : ImGuiDragDropFlags = ImGuiDragDropFlags.new(0)) : ImGuiPayload?

def.name: accept_drag_drop_payload
def.body:
result = LibImGui.igAcceptDragDropPayload(type, flags)
result ? ImGuiPayload.new(result) : nil

class_methods.name: align_text_to_frame_padding
class_methods.doc:
vertically align upcoming text baseline to FramePadding.y so that it will align properly to regularly framed items (call if you have text on a line before a framed item)

[ImGui::AlignTextToFramePadding()](https://github.com/ocornut/imgui/blob/v1.81/imgui.h#L422)
class_methods.summary: vertically align upcoming text baseline to FramePadding.y so that it will align properly to regularly framed items (call if you have text on a line before a framed item)

class_methods.name: calc_item_width
class_methods.doc:
width of item given pushed settings and current cursor position. NOT necessarily the width of last item unlike most 'Item' functions.

[ImGui::CalcItemWidth()](https://github.com/ocornut/imgui/blob/v1.81/imgui.h#L384)
class_methods.summary: width of item given pushed settings and current cursor position.

class_methods.name: calc_list_clipping
class_methods.doc:
calculate coarse clipping for large list of evenly sized items. Prefer using the ImGuiListClipper higher-level helper if you can.

[ImGui::CalcListClipping()](https://github.com/ocornut/imgui/blob/v1.81/imgui.h#L810)
class_methods.summary: calculate coarse clipping for large list of evenly sized items.

def.name: calc_list_clipping
def.body:
LibImGui.igCalcListClipping(items_count, items_height, out out_items_display_start, out out_items_display_end)
{out_items_display_start, out_items_display_end}

class_methods.name: calc_text_size
class_methods.args_string: (text : Bytes | String, hide_text_after_double_hash : Bool = false, wrap_width : Float32 = -1.0) : ImGui::ImVec2
class_methods.args_html: (text : Bytes | String, hide_text_after_double_hash : Bool = false, wrap_width : Float32 = -1.0) : ImGui::ImVec2

def.name: calc_text_size
def.body:
LibImGui.igCalcTextSize(out p_out, text, (text.to_unsafe + text.bytesize), hide_text_after_double_hash, wrap_width)
p_out

class_methods.name: capture_keyboard_from_app
class_methods.doc:
attention: misleading name! manually override io.WantCaptureKeyboard flag next frame (said flag is entirely left for your application to handle). e.g. force capture keyboard when your widget is being hovered. This is equivalent to setting "io.WantCaptureKeyboard = want_capture_keyboard_value"; after the next NewFrame() call.

[ImGui::CaptureKeyboardFromApp()](https://github.com/ocornut/imgui/blob/v1.81/imgui.h#L831)
class_methods.summary: attention: misleading name! manually override io.WantCaptureKeyboard flag next frame (said flag is entirely left for your application to handle).

class_methods.name: capture_mouse_from_app
class_methods.doc:
attention: misleading name! manually override io.WantCaptureMouse flag next frame (said flag is entirely left for your application to handle). This is equivalent to setting "io.WantCaptureMouse = want_capture_mouse_value;" after the next NewFrame() call.

[ImGui::CaptureMouseFromApp()](https://github.com/ocornut/imgui/blob/v1.81/imgui.h#L851)
class_methods.summary: attention: misleading name! manually override io.WantCaptureMouse flag next frame (said flag is entirely left for your application to handle).

def.name: color_edit3
def.body: LibImGui.igColorEdit3(label, col.is_a?(Indexable) ? (col.size == 3 ? col.to_unsafe : raise(ArgumentError.new("Slice has wrong size #{col.size} (want 3)"))) : col.as(::Pointer(Float32)), flags)

def.name: combo
def.body:
typeof(current_item.value.to_i32)
current_item = current_item.as(::Pointer(Int32))
LibImGui.igComboFnBoolPtr(label, current_item, ->(data, idx, out_text) do
  val = (data.as(::Pointer(typeof(block)))).value.call(idx)
  if val
    out_text.value = val.to_unsafe
    true
  else
    false
  end
end, pointerof(block), items_count, popup_max_height_in_items)

class_methods.name: input_text
class_methods.args_string: (label : String, buf : TextBuffer, flags : ImGuiInputTextFlags = ImGuiInputTextFlags.new(0), &block : ImGuiInputTextCallbackData -> Int32) : Bool
class_methods.args_html: (label : String, buf : TextBuffer, flags : ImGuiInputTextFlags = ImGuiInputTextFlags.new(0), &block : ImGuiInputTextCallbackData -> Int32) : Bool

def.name: input_text
def.body:
if block == NULL_CALLBACK
  block = nil
end
user_data = {buf, block}
LibImGui.igInputText(label, buf, buf.capacity, flags, ->(data) do
  data = ImGuiInputTextCallbackData.new(data)
  buf2, block2 = (data.user_data.as(::Pointer(typeof(user_data)))).value
  if data.event_flag == ImGuiInputTextFlags::CallbackResize
    buf2.resize(data.buf_text_len)
    data.buf = buf2.to_unsafe
  end
  block2 ? block2.call(data) : 0
end, pointerof(user_data))

def.name: list_box
def.body:
typeof(current_item.value.to_i32)
current_item = current_item.as(::Pointer(Int32))
LibImGui.igListBoxFnBoolPtr(label, current_item, ->(data, idx, out_text) do
  val = (data.as(::Pointer(typeof(block)))).value.call(idx)
  if val
    out_text.value = val.to_unsafe
    true
  else
    false
  end
end, pointerof(block), items_count, height_in_items)

instance_methods.name: clip_rect
instance_methods.doc:
4*4
Clipping rectangle (x1, y1, x2, y2). Subtract ImDrawData->DisplayPos to get clipping rectangle in "viewport" coordinates

instance_methods.name: elem_count
instance_methods.doc:
4
Number of indices (multiple of 3) to be rendered as triangles. Vertices are stored in the callee ImDrawList's vtx_buffer[] array, indices in idx_buffer[].
instance_methods.summary: 4 Number of indices (multiple of 3) to be rendered as triangles.

instance_methods.name: idx_offset
instance_methods.doc:
4
Start offset in index buffer. Always equal to sum of ElemCount drawn so far.

instance_methods.name: texture_id
instance_methods.doc:
4-8
User-provided texture ID. Set by user in ImfontAtlas::SetTexID() for fonts or passed to Image*() functions. Ignore if never using images or multiple fonts atlas.

instance_methods.name: user_callback
instance_methods.doc:
4-8
If != NULL, call the function instead of rendering the vertices. clip_rect and texture_id will be set normally.
instance_methods.summary: 4-8 If != NULL, call the function instead of rendering the vertices.

instance_methods.name: user_callback_data
instance_methods.doc:
4-8
The draw callback code can access this.
instance_methods.summary: 4-8 The draw callback code can access this.

instance_methods.name: vtx_offset
instance_methods.doc:
4
Start offset in vertex buffer. ImGuiBackendFlags_RendererHasVtxOffset: always 0, otherwise may be >0 to support meshes larger than 64K vertices with 16-bit indices.

instance_methods.name: de_index_all_buffers
instance_methods.doc:
Helper to convert all buffers from indexed to non-indexed, in case you cannot render indexed. Note: this is slow and most likely a waste of resources. Always prefer indexed rendering!

[ImDrawData::DeIndexAllBuffers()](https://github.com/ocornut/imgui/blob/v1.81/imgui.h#L2485)
instance_methods.summary: Helper to convert all buffers from indexed to non-indexed, in case you cannot render indexed.

instance_methods.name: display_pos
instance_methods.doc: Top-left position of the viewport to render (== top-left of the orthogonal projection matrix to use) (== GetMainViewport()->Pos for the main viewport, == (0.0) in most single-viewport applications)
instance_methods.summary: Top-left position of the viewport to render (== top-left of the orthogonal projection matrix to use) (== GetMainViewport()->Pos for the main viewport, == (0.0) in most single-viewport applications)

instance_methods.name: display_size
instance_methods.doc: Size of the viewport to render (== GetMainViewport()->Size for the main viewport, == io.DisplaySize in most single-viewport applications)
instance_methods.summary: Size of the viewport to render (== GetMainViewport()->Size for the main viewport, == io.DisplaySize in most single-viewport applications)

instance_methods.name: framebuffer_scale
instance_methods.doc: Amount of pixels for each unit of DisplaySize. Based on io.DisplayFramebufferScale. Generally (1,1) on normal display, (2,2) on OSX with Retina display.
instance_methods.summary: Amount of pixels for each unit of DisplaySize.

instance_methods.name: scale_clip_rects
instance_methods.doc:
Helper to scale the ClipRect field of each ImDrawCmd. Use if your final output buffer is at a different scale than Dear ImGui expects, or if there is a difference between your window resolution and framebuffer resolution.

[ImDrawData::ScaleClipRects()](https://github.com/ocornut/imgui/blob/v1.81/imgui.h#L2486)
instance_methods.summary: Helper to scale the ClipRect field of each ImDrawCmd.

instance_methods.summary: For convenience, sum of all ImDrawList's IdxBuffer.Size

instance_methods.doc: For convenience, sum of all ImDrawList's VtxBuffer.Size
instance_methods.summary: For convenience, sum of all ImDrawList's VtxBuffer.Size

def.name: add_callback
def.body:
LibImGui.ImDrawList_AddCallback(self, ->(parent_list, cmd) do
  block2 = Box(typeof(block)).unbox(cmd.value.user_callback_data)
  block2.call(ImDrawList.new(parent_list), ImDrawCmd.new(cmd))
end, Box.box(block))

instance_methods.name: push_clip_rect
instance_methods.doc:
Render-level scissoring. This is passed down to your render function but not used for CPU-side coarse clipping. Prefer using higher-level ImGui::PushClipRect() to affect logic (hit-testing and widget culling)

constants.name: NoBakedLines
constants.doc: Don't build thick line textures into the atlas (save a little texture memory). The AntiAliasedLinesUseTex features uses them, otherwise they will be rendered using polygons (more expensive for CPU/GPU).

instance_methods.name: merge_mode
instance_methods.doc:
false
Merge into previous ImFont, so you can combine multiple inputs font into one ImFont (e.g. ASCII font + icons + Japanese glyphs). You may want to use GlyphOffset.y when merge font of different heights.
instance_methods.summary: false Merge into previous ImFont, so you can combine multiple inputs font into one ImFont (e.g.

instance_methods.name: name
instance_methods.doc: Name (strictly to ease debugging)

instance_methods.name: oversample_h
instance_methods.doc:
3
Rasterize at higher quality for sub-pixel positioning. Note the difference between 2 and 3 is minimal so you can reduce this to 2 to save memory. Read https://github.com/nothings/stb/blob/master/tests/oversample/README.md for details.
instance_methods.summary: 3 Rasterize at higher quality for sub-pixel positioning.

instance_methods.name: oversample_v
instance_methods.doc:
1
Rasterize at higher quality for sub-pixel positioning. This is not really useful as we don't use sub-pixel positions on the Y axis.
instance_methods.summary: 1 Rasterize at higher quality for sub-pixel positioning.

instance_methods.name: pixel_snap_h
instance_methods.doc:
false
Align every glyph to pixel boundary. Useful e.g. if you are merging a non-pixel aligned font with the default font. If enabled, you can set OversampleH/V to 1.
instance_methods.summary: false Align every glyph to pixel boundary.

instance_methods.name: rasterizer_multiply
instance_methods.doc:
1.0f
Brighten (>1.0f) or darken (<1.0f) font output. Brightening small fonts may be a good workaround to make them more readable.

instance_methods.name: size_pixels
instance_methods.doc: Size in pixels for rasterizer (more or less maps to the resulting font height).
instance_methods.summary: Size in pixels for rasterizer (more or less maps to the resulting font height).

constructors.args_string: (colored : UInt32, visible : UInt32, codepoint : UInt32, advance_x : Float32, x0 : Float32, y0 : Float32, x1 : Float32, y1 : Float32, u0 : Float32, v0 : Float32, u1 : Float32, v1 : Float32)
constructors.args_html: (colored : UInt32, visible : UInt32, codepoint : UInt32, advance_x : Float32, x0 : Float32, y0 : Float32, x1 : Float32, y1 : Float32, u0 : Float32, v0 : Float32, u1 : Float32, v1 : Float32)

def.name: new
def.body:
_ = allocate
_.initialize(colored, visible, codepoint, advance_x, x0, y0, x1, y1, u0, v0, u1, v1)
if _.responds_to?(:finalize)
  ::GC.add_finalizer(_)
end
_

constants.name: NavEnableKeyboard
constants.doc: Master keyboard navigation enable flag. NewFrame() will automatically fill io.NavInputs[] based on io.KeysDown[].
constants.summary: Master keyboard navigation enable flag.

constants.name: NavEnableGamepad
constants.doc: Master gamepad navigation enable flag. This is mostly to instruct your imgui backend to fill io.NavInputs[]. Backend also needs to set ImGuiBackendFlags_HasGamepad.
constants.summary: Master gamepad navigation enable flag.

constants.name: NavEnableSetMousePos
constants.doc: Instruct navigation to move the mouse cursor. May be useful on TV/console systems where moving a virtual mouse is awkward. Will update io.MousePos and set io.WantSetMousePos=true. If enabled you MUST honor io.WantSetMousePos requests in your backend, otherwise ImGui will react as if the mouse is jumping around back and forth.
constants.summary: Instruct navigation to move the mouse cursor.

constants.name: NavNoCaptureKeyboard
constants.doc: Instruct navigation to not set the io.WantCaptureKeyboard flag when io.NavActive is set.
constants.summary: Instruct navigation to not set the io.WantCaptureKeyboard flag when io.NavActive is set.

constants.name: NoMouse
constants.doc: Instruct imgui to clear mouse position/buttons in NewFrame(). This allows ignoring the mouse information set by the backend.
constants.summary: Instruct imgui to clear mouse position/buttons in NewFrame().

constants.name: NoMouseCursorChange
constants.doc: Instruct backend to not alter mouse cursor shape and visibility. Use if the backend cursor changes are interfering with yours and you don't want to use SetMouseCursor() to change mouse cursor. You may want to honor requests from imgui by reading GetMouseCursor() yourself instead.
constants.summary: Instruct backend to not alter mouse cursor shape and visibility.

constants.name: SourceNoPreviewTooltip
constants.doc: By default, a successful call to BeginDragDropSource opens a tooltip so you can display a preview or description of the source contents. This flag disable this behavior.
constants.summary: By default, a successful call to BeginDragDropSource opens a tooltip so you can display a preview or description of the source contents.

constants.name: SourceNoDisableHover
constants.doc: By default, when dragging we clear data so that IsItemHovered() will return false, to avoid subsequent user code submitting tooltips. This flag disable this behavior so you can still call IsItemHovered() on the source item.
constants.summary: By default, when dragging we clear data so that IsItemHovered() will return false, to avoid subsequent user code submitting tooltips.

constants.name: SourceNoHoldToOpenOthers
constants.doc: Disable the behavior that allows to open tree nodes and collapsing header by holding over them while dragging a source item.
constants.summary: Disable the behavior that allows to open tree nodes and collapsing header by holding over them while dragging a source item.

constants.name: SourceAllowNullID
constants.doc: Allow items such as Text(), Image() that have no unique identifier to be used as drag source, by manufacturing a temporary identifier based on their window-relative position. This is extremely unusual within the dear imgui ecosystem and so we made it explicit.
constants.summary: Allow items such as Text(), Image() that have no unique identifier to be used as drag source, by manufacturing a temporary identifier based on their window-relative position.

constants.name: SourceExtern
constants.doc: External source (from outside of dear imgui), won't attempt to read current item/window info. Will always return true. Only one Extern source can be active simultaneously.
constants.summary: External source (from outside of dear imgui), won't attempt to read current item/window info.

constants.name: SourceAutoExpirePayload
constants.doc: Automatically expire the payload if the source cease to be submitted (otherwise payloads are persisting while being dragged)
constants.summary: Automatically expire the payload if the source cease to be submitted (otherwise payloads are persisting while being dragged)

constants.name: AcceptBeforeDelivery
constants.doc: AcceptDragDropPayload() will returns true even before the mouse button is released. You can then call IsDelivery() to test if the payload needs to be delivered.
constants.summary: AcceptDragDropPayload() will returns true even before the mouse button is released.

constants.name: AcceptNoDrawDefaultRect
constants.doc: Do not draw the default highlight rectangle when hovering over target.
constants.summary: Do not draw the default highlight rectangle when hovering over target.

constants.name: AcceptNoPreviewTooltip
constants.doc: Request hiding the BeginDragDropSource tooltip from the BeginDragDropTarget site.
constants.summary: Request hiding the BeginDragDropSource tooltip from the BeginDragDropTarget site.

constants.name: AcceptPeekOnly
constants.doc: For peeking ahead and inspecting the payload before delivery.
constants.summary: For peeking ahead and inspecting the payload before delivery.

constants.name: ChildWindows

constants.doc: Allow 0123456789ABCDEFabcdef

constants.summary: Filter out spaces, tabs

constants.name: AutoSelectAll
constants.doc: Select entire text when first taking mouse focus
constants.summary: Select entire text when first taking mouse focus

constants.doc: Return 'true' when Enter is pressed (as opposed to every time the value was modified). Consider looking at the IsItemDeactivatedAfterEdit() function.

constants.doc: Callback on pressing TAB (for completion handling)
constants.summary: Callback on pressing TAB (for completion handling)

constants.name: CallbackHistory
constants.doc: Callback on pressing Up/Down arrows (for history handling)
constants.summary: Callback on pressing Up/Down arrows (for history handling)

constants.name: CallbackAlways
constants.doc: Callback on each iteration. User code may query cursor position, modify text buffer.
constants.summary: Callback on each iteration.

constants.name: CallbackCharFilter
constants.doc: Callback on character inputs to replace or discard them. Modify 'EventChar' to replace or discard, or return 1 in callback to discard.
constants.summary: Callback on character inputs to replace or discard them.

constants.name: AllowTabInput
constants.doc: Pressing TAB input a '\t' character into the text field
constants.summary: Pressing TAB input a '\t' character into the text field

constants.name: CtrlEnterForNewLine
constants.doc: In multi-line mode, unfocus with Enter, add new line with Ctrl+Enter (default is opposite: unfocus with Ctrl+Enter, add line with Enter).
constants.summary: In multi-line mode, unfocus with Enter, add new line with Ctrl+Enter (default is opposite: unfocus with Ctrl+Enter, add line with Enter).

constants.name: NoHorizontalScroll
constants.doc: Disable following the cursor horizontally
constants.summary: Disable following the cursor horizontally

constants.name: AlwaysInsertMode
constants.doc: Insert mode
constants.summary: Insert mode

constants.name: ReadOnly

constants.summary: Read-only mode

constants.name: Password
constants.doc: Password mode, display all characters as '*'
constants.summary: Password mode, display all characters as '*'

constants.name: NoUndoRedo
constants.doc: Disable undo/redo. Note that input text owns the text data while active, if you want to provide your own undo/redo stack you need e.g. to call ClearActiveID().
constants.summary: Disable undo/redo.

constants.name: CharsScientific
constants.doc: Allow 0123456789.+-*/eE (Scientific notation input)
constants.summary: Allow 0123456789.+-*/eE (Scientific notation input)

constants.name: CallbackResize
constants.doc: Callback on buffer capacity changes request (beyond 'buf_size' parameter value), allowing the string to grow. Notify when the string wants to be resized (for string types which hold a cache of their Size). You will be provided a new BufSize in the callback and NEED to honor it. (see misc/cpp/imgui_stdlib.h for an example of using this)
constants.summary: Callback on buffer capacity changes request (beyond 'buf_size' parameter value), allowing the string to grow.

constants.name: CallbackEdit
constants.doc: Callback on any edit (note that InputText() already returns true on edit, the callback is useful mainly to manipulate the underlying buffer while focus is active)
constants.summary: Callback on any edit (note that InputText() already returns true on edit, the callback is useful mainly to manipulate the underlying buffer while focus is active)

constants.summary: For internal use by InputTextMultiline()

instance_methods.doc:
= defined(__APPLE__)
OS X style: Text editing cursor movement using Alt instead of Ctrl, Shortcuts using Cmd/Super instead of Ctrl, Line/Text Start and End using Cmd+Arrows instead of Home/End, Double click selects by word instead of selecting whole text, Multi-selection in lists uses Cmd/Super instead of Ctrl.
instance_methods.summary: = defined(__APPLE__) OS X style: Text editing cursor movement using Alt instead of Ctrl, Shortcuts using Cmd/Super instead of Ctrl, Line/Text Start and End using Cmd+Arrows instead of Home/End, Double click selects by word instead of selecting whole text, Multi-selection in lists uses Cmd/Super instead of Ctrl.

(e.g. unclicked mouse is hovering over an imgui window, widget is active, mouse was clicked over an imgui window, etc.).

constructors.name: new
constructors.args_string: (display_start : Int32, display_end : Int32, items_count : Int32, step_no : Int32, items_frozen : Int32, items_height : Float32, start_pos_y : Float32)
constructors.args_html: (display_start : Int32, display_end : Int32, items_count : Int32, step_no : Int32, items_frozen : Int32, items_height : Float32, start_pos_y : Float32)

def.name: new
def.body:
_ = allocate
_.initialize(display_start, display_end, items_count, step_no, items_frozen, items_height, start_pos_y)
if _.responds_to?(:finalize)
  ::GC.add_finalizer(_)
end
_

instance_methods.name: anti_aliased_fill

instance_methods.doc: Enable anti-aliased lines/borders. Disable if you are really tight on CPU/GPU. Latched at the beginning of the frame (copied to ImDrawList).

instance_methods.name: anti_aliased_lines_use_tex
instance_methods.doc: Enable anti-aliased lines/borders using textures where possible. Require backend to render with bilinear filtering. Latched at the beginning of the frame (copied to ImDrawList).

instance_methods.summary: Expand reactive bounding box for touch-based system where touch position is not accurate enough.

instance_methods.name: window_border_size
instance_methods.doc: Thickness of border around windows. Generally set to 0.0f or 1.0f. (Other values are not well tested and more CPU/GPU costly).

instance_methods.name: window_rounding
instance_methods.doc: Radius of window corners rounding. Set to 0.0f to have rectangular windows. Large values tend to lead to variety of artifacts and are not recommended.

instance_methods.name: window_title_align
instance_methods.doc: Alignment for title bar text. Defaults to (0.0f,0.5f) for left-aligned,vertically centered.

  ::GC.add_finalizer(_)

instance_methods.name: size
instance_methods.doc: Returns the number of elements in this container.
instance_methods.summary: Returns the number of elements in this container.

instance_methods.name: unsafe_fetch
instance_methods.doc:
Returns the element at the given *index*, without doing any bounds check.

`Indexable` makes sure to invoke this method with *index* in `0...size`,
so converting negative indices to positive ones is not needed here.

Clients never invoke this method directly. Instead, they access
elements with `#[](index)` and `#[]?(index)`.

This method should only be directly invoked if you are absolutely
sure the index is in bounds, to avoid a bounds check for a small boost
of performance.
instance_methods.summary: Returns the element at the given index, without doing any bounds check.

instance_methods.name: to_s
instance_methods.doc:
Returns a string representation of this object.

Descendants must usually **not** override this method. Instead,
they must override `to_s(io)`, which must append to the given
IO object.
instance_methods.summary: Returns a string representation of this object.

instance_methods.name: write
instance_methods.doc:
Writes the contents of *slice* into this `IO`.

```
io = IO::Memory.new
slice = Bytes.new(4) { |i| ('a'.ord + i).to_u8 }
io.write(slice)
io.to_s # => "abcd"
```

def.name: write
def.body:
old_size = bytesize
resize(bytesize + slice.bytesize)
slice.copy_to(@buf + old_size, slice.bytesize)

constants.summary: Enable anti-aliased lines/borders (*2 the number of triangles for 1.0f wide line or lines thin enough to be drawn using textures, otherwise *3 the number of triangles)

constants.summary: Enable anti-aliased lines/borders using textures when possible.

constants.name: NoBakedLines
constants.doc: Don't build thick line textures into the atlas (save a little texture memory). The AntiAliasedLinesUseTex features uses them, otherwise they will be rendered using polygons (more expensive for CPU/GPU).

constants.summary: Backend Platform supports honoring GetMouseCursor() value to change the OS cursor shape.

constants.doc: Backend Platform supports io.WantSetMousePos requests to reposition the OS mouse position (only used if ImGuiConfigFlags_NavEnableSetMousePos is set).
constants.summary: Backend Platform supports io.WantSetMousePos requests to reposition the OS mouse position (only used if ImGuiConfigFlags_NavEnableSetMousePos is set).

constants.summary: Backend Renderer supports ImDrawCmd::VtxOffset.

constants.summary: Master gamepad navigation enable flag.

constants.doc: Master keyboard navigation enable flag. NewFrame() will automatically fill io.NavInputs[] based on io.KeysDown[].

constants.name: NavEnableSetMousePos
constants.doc: Instruct navigation to move the mouse cursor. May be useful on TV/console systems where moving a virtual mouse is awkward. Will update io.MousePos and set io.WantSetMousePos=true. If enabled you MUST honor io.WantSetMousePos requests in your backend, otherwise ImGui will react as if the mouse is jumping around back and forth.

constants.name: NavNoCaptureKeyboard
constants.doc: Instruct navigation to not set the io.WantCaptureKeyboard flag when io.NavActive is set.
constants.summary: Instruct navigation to not set the io.WantCaptureKeyboard flag when io.NavActive is set.

constants.name: NoMouse
constants.doc: Instruct imgui to clear mouse position/buttons in NewFrame(). This allows ignoring the mouse information set by the backend.
constants.summary: Instruct imgui to clear mouse position/buttons in NewFrame().

constants.name: NoMouseCursorChange
constants.doc: Instruct backend to not alter mouse cursor shape and visibility. Use if the backend cursor changes are interfering with yours and you don't want to use SetMouseCursor() to change mouse cursor. You may want to honor requests from imgui by reading GetMouseCursor() yourself instead.
constants.summary: Instruct backend to not alter mouse cursor shape and visibility.

constants.doc: Allow items such as Text(), Image() that have no unique identifier to be used as drag source, by manufacturing a temporary identifier based on their window-relative position. This is extremely unusual within the dear imgui ecosystem and so we made it explicit.
constants.summary: Allow items such as Text(), Image() that have no unique identifier to be used as drag source, by manufacturing a temporary identifier based on their window-relative position.

constants.doc: Automatically expire the payload if the source cease to be submitted (otherwise payloads are persisting while being dragged)
constants.summary: Automatically expire the payload if the source cease to be submitted (otherwise payloads are persisting while being dragged)

constants.doc: External source (from outside of dear imgui), won't attempt to read current item/window info. Will always return true. Only one Extern source can be active simultaneously.
constants.summary: External source (from outside of dear imgui), won't attempt to read current item/window info.

constants.name: SourceNoDisableHover
constants.doc: By default, when dragging we clear data so that IsItemHovered() will return false, to avoid subsequent user code submitting tooltips. This flag disable this behavior so you can still call IsItemHovered() on the source item.
constants.summary: By default, when dragging we clear data so that IsItemHovered() will return false, to avoid subsequent user code submitting tooltips.

constants.name: SourceNoHoldToOpenOthers
constants.doc: Disable the behavior that allows to open tree nodes and collapsing header by holding over them while dragging a source item.
constants.summary: Disable the behavior that allows to open tree nodes and collapsing header by holding over them while dragging a source item.

constants.name: SourceNoPreviewTooltip
constants.doc: By default, a successful call to BeginDragDropSource opens a tooltip so you can display a preview or description of the source contents. This flag disable this behavior.
constants.summary: By default, a successful call to BeginDragDropSource opens a tooltip so you can display a preview or description of the source contents.

constants.summary: IsWindowFocused(): Return true if any children of the window is focused

constants.summary: Select entire text when first taking mouse focus

constants.doc: Callback on character inputs to replace or discard them. Modify 'EventChar' to replace or discard, or return 1 in callback to discard.

constants.summary: Callback on pressing Up/Down arrows (for history handling)

constants.name: CallbackResize
constants.doc: Callback on buffer capacity changes request (beyond 'buf_size' parameter value), allowing the string to grow. Notify when the string wants to be resized (for string types which hold a cache of their Size). You will be provided a new BufSize in the callback and NEED to honor it. (see misc/cpp/imgui_stdlib.h for an example of using this)
constants.summary: Callback on buffer capacity changes request (beyond 'buf_size' parameter value), allowing the string to grow.

constants.summary: Allow 0123456789ABCDEFabcdef

constants.summary: [ALPHA] Disable vertical borders in columns Body (borders will always appears in Headers).

constants.summary: [ALPHA] Disable vertical borders in columns Body until hovered for resize (borders will always appears in Headers).

constants.summary: Disable clipping rectangle for every individual columns (reduce draw command count, items will be able to overflow into other columns).

constants.summary: Make outer height stop exactly at outer_size.y (prevent auto-extending table past the limit).

constants.summary: Disable distributing remainder width to stretched columns (width allocation on a 100-wide table with 3 columns: Without this flag: 33,33,34.

constants.summary: Ensure child windows without border uses style.WindowPadding (ignored by default for non-bordered child windows, because more convenient)

constants.name: AlwaysVerticalScrollbar
constants.doc: Always show vertical scrollbar (even if ContentSize.y < Size.y)
constants.summary: Always show vertical scrollbar (even if ContentSize.y < Size.y)

constants.name: ChildMenu
constants.doc: Don't use! For internal use by BeginMenu()
constants.summary: Don't use! For internal use by BeginMenu()

constants.name: ChildWindow
constants.doc: Don't use! For internal use by BeginChild()
constants.summary: Don't use! For internal use by BeginChild()

constants.name: HorizontalScrollbar
constants.doc: Allow horizontal scrollbar to appear (off by default). You may use SetNextWindowContentSize(ImVec2(width,0.0f)); prior to calling Begin() to specify width. Read code in imgui_demo in the "Horizontal Scrolling" section.
constants.summary: Allow horizontal scrollbar to appear (off by default).

constants.name: MenuBar
constants.doc: Has a menu-bar
constants.summary: Has a menu-bar

constants.name: Modal
constants.doc: Don't use! For internal use by BeginPopupModal()
constants.summary: Don't use! For internal use by BeginPopupModal()

constants.name: NavFlattened
constants.doc: [BETA] Allow gamepad/keyboard navigation to cross over parent border to this child (only use on child that have no scrolling!)
constants.summary: [BETA] Allow gamepad/keyboard navigation to cross over parent border to this child (only use on child that have no scrolling!)

constants.name: NoBackground
constants.doc: Disable drawing background color (WindowBg, etc.) and outside border. Similar as using SetNextWindowBgAlpha(0.0f).
constants.summary: Disable drawing background color (WindowBg, etc.) and outside border.

constants.name: NoBringToFrontOnFocus
constants.doc: Disable bringing window to front when taking focus (e.g. clicking on it or programmatically giving it focus)
constants.summary: Disable bringing window to front when taking focus (e.g.

constants.name: NoCollapse
constants.doc: Disable user collapsing window by double-clicking on it
constants.summary: Disable user collapsing window by double-clicking on it

constants.name: NoFocusOnAppearing
constants.doc: Disable taking focus when transitioning from hidden to visible state
constants.summary: Disable taking focus when transitioning from hidden to visible state

constants.name: NoMouseInputs
constants.doc: Disable catching mouse, hovering test with pass through.
constants.summary: Disable catching mouse, hovering test with pass through.

constants.name: NoMove
constants.doc: Disable user moving the window
constants.summary: Disable user moving the window

constants.name: NoNavFocus
constants.doc: No focusing toward this window with gamepad/keyboard navigation (e.g. skipped by CTRL+TAB)
constants.summary: No focusing toward this window with gamepad/keyboard navigation (e.g.

constants.name: NoNavInputs
constants.doc: No gamepad/keyboard navigation within the window
constants.summary: No gamepad/keyboard navigation within the window

constants.name: NoResize
constants.doc: Disable user resizing with the lower-right grip
constants.summary: Disable user resizing with the lower-right grip

constants.name: NoSavedSettings
constants.doc: Never load/save settings in .ini file
constants.summary: Never load/save settings in .ini file

constants.name: NoScrollbar
constants.doc: Disable scrollbars (window can still scroll with mouse or programmatically)
constants.summary: Disable scrollbars (window can still scroll with mouse or programmatically)

constants.name: NoScrollWithMouse
constants.doc: Disable user vertically scrolling with mouse wheel. On child window, mouse wheel will be forwarded to the parent unless NoScrollbar is also set.
constants.summary: Disable user vertically scrolling with mouse wheel.

constants.name: NoTitleBar
constants.doc: Disable title-bar
constants.summary: Disable title-bar

constants.name: Popup
constants.doc: Don't use! For internal use by BeginPopup()
constants.summary: Don't use! For internal use by BeginPopup()

constants.name: Tooltip
constants.doc: Don't use! For internal use by BeginTooltip()
constants.summary: Don't use! For internal use by BeginTooltip()

constants.name: UnsavedDocument
constants.doc: Append '*' to title without affecting the ID, as a convenience to avoid using the ### operator. When used in a tab/docking context, tab is selected on closure and closure is deferred by one frame to allow code to cancel the closure (with a confirmation popup, etc.) without flicker.
constants.summary: Append '*' to title without affecting the ID, as a convenience to avoid using the ### operator.

def.name: edit_table_sizing_flags
def.body:
policies = [EnumDesc.new(ImGuiTableFlags::None, "Default", "Use default sizing policy:\n- ImGuiTableFlags_SizingFixedFit if ScrollX is on or if host window has ImGuiWindowFlags_AlwaysAutoResize.\n- ImGuiTableFlags_SizingStretchSame otherwise."), EnumDesc.new(ImGuiTableFlags::SizingFixedFit, "ImGuiTableFlags_SizingFixedFit", "Columns default to _WidthFixed (if resizable) or _WidthAuto (if not resizable), matching contents width."), EnumDesc.new(ImGuiTableFlags::SizingFixedSame, "ImGuiTableFlags_SizingFixedSame", "Columns are all the same width, matching the maximum contents width.\nImplicitly disable ImGuiTableFlags_Resizable and enable ImGuiTableFlags_NoKeepColumnsVisible."), EnumDesc.new(ImGuiTableFlags::SizingStretchProp, "ImGuiTableFlags_SizingStretchProp", "Columns default to _WidthStretch with weights proportional to their widths."), EnumDesc.new(ImGuiTableFlags::SizingStretchSame, "ImGuiTableFlags_SizingStretchSame", "Columns default to _WidthStretch with same weights.")]
idx = policies.index do |pol|
  pol.value == (p_flags.value & ImGuiTableFlags::SizingMask_)
end || policies.size
preview_text = (idx < policies.size) ? policies[idx].name[(idx > 0 ? "ImGuiTableFlags".size : 0)..] : ""
if ImGui.begin_combo("Sizing Policy", preview_text)
  policies.size.times do |n|
    if ImGui.selectable(policies[n].name, idx == n)
      p_flags.value = (p_flags.value & (~ImGuiTableFlags::SizingMask_)) | policies[n].value
    end
  end
  ImGui.end_combo
end
ImGui.same_line
ImGui.text_disabled("(?)")
if ImGui.is_item_hovered
  ImGui.begin_tooltip
  ImGui.push_text_wrap_pos(ImGui.get_font_size * 50.0_f32)
  policies.size.times do |m|
    ImGui.separator
    ImGui.text("%s:", policies[m].name)
    ImGui.separator
    ImGui.set_cursor_pos_x(ImGui.get_cursor_pos_x + (ImGui.get_style.indent_spacing * 0.5_f32))
    ImGui.text_unformatted(policies[m].tooltip)
  end
  ImGui.pop_text_wrap_pos
  ImGui.end_tooltip
end

def.name: node_font
def.body:
io = ImGui.get_io
style = ImGui.get_style
font_details_opened = ImGui.tree_node(font, "Font: \"%s\"\n%.2f px, %d glyphs, %d file(s)", font.config_data ? font.config_data[0].name : "", font.font_size, font.glyphs.size, font.config_data_count)
ImGui.same_line
if ImGui.small_button("Set as default")
  io.font_default = font
end
if !font_details_opened
  return
end
ImGui.push_font(font)
ImGui.text("The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog")
ImGui.pop_font
ImGui.drag_float("Font scale", pointerof(font.scale), 0.005_f32, 0.3_f32, 2.0_f32, "%.1f")
ImGui.same_line
help_marker(((("Note than the default embedded font is NOT meant to be scaled.\n\n" + "Font are currently rendered into bitmaps at a given size at the time of building the atlas. ") + "You may oversample them to get some flexibility with scaling. ") + "You can also render at multiple sizes and select which one to use at runtime.\n\n") + "(Glimmer of hope: the atlas system will be rewritten in the future to make scaling more flexible.)")
ImGui.text("Ascent: %f, Descent: %f, Height: %f", font.ascent, font.descent, font.ascent - font.descent)
ImGui.text("Fallback character: '%c' (U+%04X)", font.fallback_char, font.fallback_char)
ImGui.text("Ellipsis character: '%c' (U+%04X)", font.ellipsis_char, font.ellipsis_char)
surface_sqrt = (Math.sqrt(font.metrics_total_surface)).to_i
ImGui.text("Texture Area: about %d px ~%dx%d px", font.metrics_total_surface, surface_sqrt, surface_sqrt)
font.config_data_count.times do |config_i|
  if font.config_data
    if cfg = font.config_data[config_i]
      ImGui.bullet_text("Input %d: '%s', Oversample: (%d,%d), PixelSnapH: %d, Offset: (%.1f,%.1f)", config_i, cfg.name, cfg.oversample_h, cfg.oversample_v, cfg.pixel_snap_h, cfg.glyph_offset.x, cfg.glyph_offset.y)
    end
  end
end
if ImGui.tree_node("Glyphs", "Glyphs (%d)", font.glyphs.size)
  glyph_col = ImGui.get_color_u32(ImGuiCol::Text)
  (0_u32..Char::MAX_CODEPOINT).step(256) do |base|
    if ((base & 4095) == 0) && (font.is_glyph_range_unused(base, base + 4095))
      base = base + (4096 - 256)
      next
    end
    count = 0
    256.times do |n|
      if font.find_glyph_no_fallback((base + n).chr)
        count = count + 1
      end
    end
    if count <= 0
      next
    end
    if !(ImGui.tree_node(base.to_s, "U+%04X..u+%04X (%d %s)", base, base + 255, count, count > 1 ? "glyphs" : "glyph"))
      next
    end
    cell_size = font.font_size * 1
    cell_spacing = style.item_spacing.y
    base_pos = ImGui.get_cursor_screen_pos
    draw_list = ImGui.get_window_draw_list
    256.times do |n|
      cell_p1 = ImVec2.new(base_pos.x + ((n % 16) * (cell_size + cell_spacing)), base_pos.y + ((n // 16) * (cell_size + cell_spacing)))
      cell_p2 = ImVec2.new(cell_p1.x + cell_size, cell_p1.y + cell_size)
      glyph = font.find_glyph_no_fallback((base + n).chr)
      draw_list.add_rect(cell_p1, cell_p2, glyph ? ImGui.col32(255, 255, 255, 100) : ImGui.col32(255, 255, 255, 50))
      if glyph
        font.render_char(draw_list, cell_size, cell_p1, glyph_col, (base + n).chr)
      end
      if glyph && (ImGui.is_mouse_hovering_rect(cell_p1, cell_p2))
        ImGui.begin_tooltip
        ImGui.text("Codepoint: U+%04X", base + n)
        ImGui.separator
        ImGui.text("Visible: %d", glyph.visible)
        ImGui.text("AdvanceX: %.1f", glyph.advance_x)
        ImGui.text("Pos: (%.2f,%.2f)->(%.2f,%.2f)", glyph.x0, glyph.y0, glyph.x1, glyph.y1)
        ImGui.text("UV: (%.3f,%.3f)->(%.3f,%.3f)", glyph.u0, glyph.v0, glyph.u1, glyph.v1)
        ImGui.end_tooltip
      end
    end
    ImGui.dummy(ImVec2.new((cell_size + cell_spacing) * 16, (cell_size + cell_spacing) * 16))
    ImGui.tree_pop
  end
  ImGui.tree_pop
end
ImGui.tree_pop

def.name: notify_of_documents_closed_elsewhere
def.body:
app.documents.size.times do |doc_n|
  doc = app.documents[doc_n]
  if (!doc.open?) && doc.open_prev?
    ImGui.set_tab_item_closed(doc.name)
  end
  doc.open_prev = doc.open?
end

def.name: show_about_window
def.body:
if !(ImGui.begin("About Dear ImGui", p_open, ImGuiWindowFlags::AlwaysAutoResize))
  ImGui.end
  return
end
ImGui.text("Dear ImGui %s", ImGui.get_version)
ImGui.separator
ImGui.text("By Omar Cornut and all Dear ImGui contributors.")
ImGui.text("Dear ImGui is licensed under the MIT License, see LICENSE for more information.")
static(show_config_info = false)
ImGui.checkbox("Config/Build Information", pointerof(show_config_info.val))
if show_config_info.val
  io = ImGui.get_io
  style = ImGui.get_style
  copy_to_clipboard = ImGui.button("Copy to clipboard")
  child_size = ImVec2.new(0, ImGui.get_text_line_height_with_spacing * 18)
  ImGui.begin_child_frame(ImGui.get_id("cfg_infos"), child_size, ImGuiWindowFlags::NoMove)
  if copy_to_clipboard
    ImGui.log_to_clipboard
    ImGui.log_text("```\n")
  end
  ImGui.text("Dear ImGui %s", ImGui.get_version)
  ImGui.separator
  ImGui.text("sizeof(size_t): %d, sizeof(ImDrawIdx): %d, sizeof(ImDrawVert): %d", sizeof(LibC::SizeT), sizeof(ImGui::ImDrawIdx), sizeof(ImDrawVert))
  ImGui.separator
  ImGui.text("io.BackendPlatformName: %s", io.backend_platform_name || "NULL")
  ImGui.text("io.BackendRendererName: %s", io.backend_renderer_name || "NULL")
  ImGui.text("io.ConfigFlags: 0x%08X", io.config_flags)
  if io.config_flags.includes?(:NavEnableKeyboard)
    ImGui.text(" NavEnableKeyboard")
  end
  if io.config_flags.includes?(:NavEnableGamepad)
    ImGui.text(" NavEnableGamepad")
  end
  if io.config_flags.includes?(:NavEnableSetMousePos)
    ImGui.text(" NavEnableSetMousePos")
  end
  if io.config_flags.includes?(:NavNoCaptureKeyboard)
    ImGui.text(" NavNoCaptureKeyboard")
  end
  if io.config_flags.includes?(:NoMouse)
    ImGui.text(" NoMouse")
  end
  if io.config_flags.includes?(:NoMouseCursorChange)
    ImGui.text(" NoMouseCursorChange")
  end
  if io.mouse_draw_cursor
    ImGui.text("io.MouseDrawCursor")
  end
  if io.config_mac_osx_behaviors
    ImGui.text("io.ConfigMacOSXBehaviors")
  end
  if io.config_input_text_cursor_blink
    ImGui.text("io.ConfigInputTextCursorBlink")
  end
  if io.config_windows_resize_from_edges
    ImGui.text("io.ConfigWindowsResizeFromEdges")
  end
  if io.config_windows_move_from_title_bar_only
    ImGui.text("io.ConfigWindowsMoveFromTitleBarOnly")
  end
  if io.config_memory_compact_timer >= 0.0_f32
    ImGui.text("io.ConfigMemoryCompactTimer = %.1f", io.config_memory_compact_timer)
  end
  ImGui.text("io.BackendFlags: 0x%08X", io.backend_flags)
  if io.backend_flags.includes?(:HasGamepad)
    ImGui.text(" HasGamepad")
  end
  if io.backend_flags.includes?(:HasMouseCursors)
    ImGui.text(" HasMouseCursors")
  end
  if io.backend_flags.includes?(:HasSetMousePos)
    ImGui.text(" HasSetMousePos")
  end
  if io.backend_flags.includes?(:RendererHasVtxOffset)
    ImGui.text(" RendererHasVtxOffset")
  end
  ImGui.separator
  ImGui.text("io.Fonts: %d fonts, Flags: 0x%08X, TexSize: %d,%d", io.fonts.fonts.size, io.fonts.flags, io.fonts.tex_width, io.fonts.tex_height)
  ImGui.text("io.DisplaySize: %.2f,%.2f", io.display_size.x, io.display_size.y)
  ImGui.text("io.DisplayFramebufferScale: %.2f,%.2f", io.display_framebuffer_scale.x, io.display_framebuffer_scale.y)
  ImGui.separator
  ImGui.text("style.WindowPadding: %.2f,%.2f", style.window_padding.x, style.window_padding.y)
  ImGui.text("style.WindowBorderSize: %.2f", style.window_border_size)
  ImGui.text("style.FramePadding: %.2f,%.2f", style.frame_padding.x, style.frame_padding.y)
  ImGui.text("style.FrameRounding: %.2f", style.frame_rounding)
  ImGui.text("style.FrameBorderSize: %.2f", style.frame_border_size)
  ImGui.text("style.ItemSpacing: %.2f,%.2f", style.item_spacing.x, style.item_spacing.y)
  ImGui.text("style.ItemInnerSpacing: %.2f,%.2f", style.item_inner_spacing.x, style.item_inner_spacing.y)
  if copy_to_clipboard
    ImGui.log_text("\n```\n")
    ImGui.log_finish
  end
  ImGui.end_child_frame
end
ImGui.end

    ImGui.end_menu
  end
  ImGui.end_menu_bar
end

ImGui.spacing

  ImGui.bullet_text("The \"Examples\" menu above leads to more demo contents.")
